title: Air-fryer baby potatoes
subtitle: Crisp-skinned new potatoes as a side dish
excerpt: Baby new potatoes pierced once, tossed in olive oil and salt, and cooked in the air fryer at 200°C for 20 to 22 minutes. The small size means the air fryer heat penetrates to the centre before the skin over-crisps. Pierce before cooking: the smooth skin traps steam and potatoes can split under pressure without a vent.
sourceNotes: Baby new potatoes have a thin, edible skin and a waxy flesh that holds together during cooking rather than breaking down. The waxy texture makes them unsuitable for mashing but well-suited to roasting and air frying. Charlotte and Maris Peer are the most common varieties in British supermarkets.

content.text: Pierce each potato once with a fork before cooking. Baby new potatoes have a very tight, smooth skin with no give. Without a small steam vent they can split violently in the air fryer as steam builds inside during cooking. One pierce is enough.

items.prepNote: baby new potatoes (Charlotte or Maris Peer), scrubbed

content.text: Pierce each {{potato}} once with a fork. Toss with {{olive-oil}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, {{rosemary}}, and {{garlic}} if using, until evenly coated. Preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 3 minutes. Add the potatoes to the basket. Cook at 200°C for 20 to 22 minutes, shaking the basket twice during cooking, until the skin is golden and slightly wrinkled and the flesh gives easily when pressed. Serve immediately.

items.fix: A single fork pierce creates a steam vent. If potatoes have already split, they are still edible but the flesh will be slightly drier

items.symptom: Skin crisped but centre still firm and underdone
items.cause: Potatoes too large for the 20-minute cook time
items.fix: Baby new potatoes larger than 3 cm across need 25 to 28 minutes. If the variety available is large, halve them before cooking to ensure even cooking

items.symptom: Garlic burning and turning bitter before potatoes are done
items.cause: Crushed garlic in direct contact with the basket at 200°C
items.fix: Add crushed garlic in the last 5 minutes of cooking rather than from the start, or use garlic powder instead for the full cook

content.text: Air-fryer baby potatoes are a versatile side that works alongside almost any protein. Serve with salmon and green beans, alongside a steak with a simple green salad, or dressed with butter and chives as part of a summer spread. Leftovers reheat well in the air fryer at 180°C for 5 minutes.
